seoTitle: How to Change Your Signature in Zendesk (Agent Guide)
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to changing your outbound email signature in Zendesk, covering personal profile edits and admin-level global template updates.
intro: A consistent, up-to-date signature helps every outbound message look professional. This guide walks through updating your personal signature in Zendesk's agent profile, plus how administrators can adjust the shared global signature template that applies account-wide.

steps.heading: Access your profile canvas
steps.body: Open your individual agent user profile from the top navigation bar.

steps.heading: Rewrite your outdated details
steps.body: Locate the sidebar signature box and update your name, title, and contact information.

steps.heading: Add Markdown styling
steps.body: Insert bold text, italics, or hyperlinks using Zendesk's Markdown styling engine to make the signature easier to read.

steps.heading: Switch to the admin path if needed
steps.body: Launch Admin Center and open the Agent Interface settings to reach the global common signature template.

steps.heading: Edit the shared card carefully
steps.body: Make account-wide changes while preserving dynamic token placeholders that pull in individual agent data.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Save your adjustments and run a public comment layout test to confirm the signature displays correctly.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to change my own signature?
faq.answer: No, agents can edit their personal signature directly from their own profile without admin access.

faq.question: What happens if I break a placeholder token in the global template?
faq.answer: Broken tokens can cause agent-specific details to display incorrectly or not populate at all, so edit them carefully.

faq.question: Can I preview my signature before it goes out to real customers?
faq.answer: Yes, running a public comment test lets you check spacing and link behavior before relying on it.

faq.question: Is Markdown required to format the signature?
faq.answer: Markdown is the supported method for adding bold text, italics, and links within the signature field.
